artPrompt: A vast dark reservoir at dusk, its oily black surface breaking into slow concentric ripples as something colossal stirs unseen below; a single slick tendril of sludge curls above the waterline among rusted pilings and dead reeds, dread thick in the heavy air, a wide landscape composition emphasizing the depth beneath. Rendered as a moody oil painting — old-master chiaroscuro, visible impasto brushwork, deep shadow, warm varnished tones.
name: The Thing Stirs
text: Each opponent flips a coin. For each opponent who loses the flip, put two sludge counters on target creature you control. For each opponent who wins, that opponent has nervously fed the thing and you put one sludge counter on it anyway.
flavorText: The water moved. Nobody threw anything in. The water moved again.
